title: Snugly Living
description: A name-state texture pack consisting of furniture and clutter for vanilla gameplay! No need for mods and/or add-ons!
body:
[![Button to the Catalog](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a62021cc56940dea301eb365cec51b14a3d826a2.png)](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/18fseTuhgr45qaIRhSvdBBuPsoW49kUWJYcUbEV31aFs/edit?usp=sharing)

A vanilla friendly texture pack that adds furniture and clutter via naming through an anvil. No need for mods/loaders, as you just need vanilla Minecraft to enjoy a snugly living! This pack in particular aims to make Minecraft feel more lived-in!

ദ്ദി(˵ •̀ ᴗ - ˵ ) ✧

![Up Next Button for Upcoming Updates Section](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/109e2dc2c99249664d6a8b4cadef40a91074eca0.png)

## **v0.3** Will Have:
### Small Outdoor Decor Update
- Picnic Basket (0, 1)
- Picnic Blanket
- Rocks (0, 1, 2, 3)
- Bench
- Watering Can (0, 1)
- Wheelbarrow
- Shovel in Dirt
- Log Pile (0, 1)

_PLEASE BE PATIENT WITH UPDATES AS I AM DOING THIS PROJECT FOR FUN! :)_

**☆ All variants will be spruce + diorite variants until v1.0+**

I plan on making versions 1.0-1.9 all about the kitchen with different wood types (except for warped and crimson) with customary stone types (smooth stone, quartz, polished granite/tuff/andesite/deepslate/blackstone) for each variants! Version 2.0+ will be about the Living Room and et cetera (0.2 ➝ 2.0, 0.3 ➝ 3.0 . . .)

<details>
<summary>FAQ</summary>

### _**Does it require Optifine/Fabric/Forge/a modloader/client?**_
>    No, this is COMPLETELY VANILLA FRIENDLY! You can have this resource pack working with regular, old basic Minecraft! It does look really nice with shaders, though...
### _**Can I use this in a YouTube video/playthrough?**_
>    Yes, but please credit the creator (me) as this texture pack is made completely by me (of course with the help of programs, like Blockbench and WinRAR)! (CC BY-ND 4.0 International)
> You can credit me by linking my linktree! ("linktr.ee/kayuni.art")
### _**Does this work with other name-state texture packs?**_
> Most likely not, a lot of CIT/name-states use similar wording (_0, _1, etc.) so most likely it's this pack or another. If you end up finding a pack that works with this one, go for it!

</details>
